title: Evening, And Maidens
content:
Now the shiades oï¿½ the elems da stratch muore an muore,
Vrom the low-zinkï¿½n zun in the west oï¿½ the sky;
Anï¿½ the mï¿½idens da stan out in clusters avore
The doors, var to chatty anï¿½ zee voï¿½ke goo by.

Anï¿½ ther cuombs be a-zet in ther bunches oï¿½ hiair,
Anï¿½ ther curdles1 da hang rounï¿½ ther necks lily-white,
Anï¿½ ther cheï¿½aks tha be ruosy, ther shoulders be biare,
Ther looks tha be merry, ther lims tha be light.

Anï¿½ the times have a been but tha cï¿½ant be noo muore
When I, too, had my jï¿½y under evemenï¿½s dim sky,
When my Fanny did stanï¿½ out wiï¿½ others avore
Her door, var to chatty anï¿½ zee voï¿½ke goo by.

Anï¿½ up there, in the green, is her own honey-zuck,
That her brother trï¿½inï¿½d up rounï¿½ her winder; anï¿½ there
Is the ruose anï¿½ the jessamy, where she did pluck
A flowï¿½r var her buzom ar bud var her hiair.

Anï¿½ zoo smile, happy mï¿½idens! var every fiace,
As the zummers da come anï¿½ the years da roll by,
Wull soon sadden, ar goo vur awoy vrom the pliace,
Ar else, likï¿½ my Fanny, wull wither anï¿½ die.

But when you be a-lost vrom the parish, some muore
Wull come on in yï¿½ur pliazen to bloom anï¿½ to die;
Anï¿½ zoo zummer wull always have mï¿½idens avore
Ther doors, var to chatty anï¿½ zee voï¿½ke goo by.

Var dï¿½aï¿½ters haï¿½ marnen when mothers haï¿½ night,
Anï¿½ thereï¿½s beauty alive when the fiairest is dead;
As when oon sparklï¿½n wiave da zink down vrom the light,
Another da come up anï¿½ catch it instead.

Zoo smile on, happy mï¿½idens! but I shall noo muore
Zee the mï¿½id I da miss under evemenï¿½s dim sky;
Anï¿½ my heart is a-touchï¿½d to zee you out avore
The doors, var to chatty and zee voï¿½ke goo by.
meaning:
About: William Barnes (22nd February 1801 - 7th October 1886) was an English writer, poet, minister, and philologist. 
He wrote over 800 poems, some in Dorset dialect and much other work including a comprehensive English grammar quoting from more than 70 different languages.
